## SECTION-187ca348-d0da-4363-92e9-5e7305025a90 @SECTION-001

> ReqIF.ChapterName: SDoc data model

### REQUIREMENT: Data model @SDOC-SRS-18

> STATUS: Active

> RATIONALE: Designing StrictDoc with a goal of having a consistent data model ensures that the tool:

1) can support a rich set of use cases,
2) model the existing documentation templates used by the industries,
3) interface well with other formats for storing documentation and requirements, e.g., ReqIF and SPDX.

> NOTES: Verification: data model diagram TBD.

### REQUIREMENT: Requirement model @SDOC-SRS-26

> STATUS: Active

### REQUIREMENT: Requirement model fields @SDOC-SRS-100

> STATUS: Active

### REQUIREMENT: Requirement model default fields @SDOC-SRS-132

> STATUS: Active

> RATIONALE: These fields are the most typical fields found in requirement documents.

### REQUIREMENT: Document model @SDOC-SRS-98

> STATUS: Active

### REQUIREMENT: Document metadata @SDOC-SRS-110

> STATUS: Active

### REQUIREMENT: Document configuration @SDOC-SRS-151

> STATUS: Active

### REQUIREMENT: Section model @SDOC-SRS-99

> STATUS: Active

> RATIONALE: "Section" corresponds to a chapter or a section in a document and helps to organize a document by grouping text nodes, requirements and other sections.

### REQUIREMENT: Free text @SDOC-SRS-135

> STATUS: Active

> RATIONALE: Documentation comprises normative components, such as uniquely identifiable elements like requirements or design items, and non-normative components, including introductory text, overview chapters, and other content. The non-normative parts help provide a general understanding for the reader but do not contribute to traceability information. StrictDoc's free text is designed to store this type of non-normative information in SDoc documents.

### REQUIREMENT: Composeable document @SDOC-SRS-109

> STATUS: Active

> RATIONALE: The logic behind the parent requirement remains fully relevant. Additionally, an alternative approach could involve using a dedicated entity, like "Fragment", to allow a Document to be composed of includable sections or document fragments. Managing composition at the Document level eliminates the need in additional entities like "Fragment", streamlining both the conceptual understanding and the practical implementation of composability.

> NOTES: The corresponding UI capability for Fragments CRUD is TBD.

### REQUIREMENT: Requirement relations @SDOC-SRS-31

> STATUS: Active

> RATIONALE: Support of both Parent and Child relations allows to build typical requirements relations such as child-to-parent and less common relations when one document can have parent links to a parent document and child links to a child document (e.g., the so-called "compliance" or "tailoring matrix" documents may use this structure).

### REQUIREMENT: Requirement relation roles @SDOC-SRS-101

> STATUS: Active

### REQUIREMENT: Inline links @SDOC-SRS-149

> STATUS: Active

> RATIONALE: The inline links do not replace the relations between SDoc nodes. Instead, an inline link could be an easy way to refer from one textual node to a chapter or a document. A common use case is "See section ABC for more details".

### REQUIREMENT: Inline anchors @SDOC-SRS-150

> STATUS: Active

> RATIONALE: Anchors allow authors to define precise reference points within SDoc node fields.
